title: The One Where Pauls The Man

jokes.text: Now you're thinking you gotta sleep with him.
jokes.setup: A massage client gave tickets to an art exhibit, creating a 'situation' the character hates
jokes.punchline: Assumes the worst possible interpretation of the gift's intentions
jokes.explanation: Solid misdirection that takes the setup in an unexpected sexual direction. Good economy and structure.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh no, what did they do? Did they give you tickets to something?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Wait, let me guess - they think you have no taste, so they're trying to educate you?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Or worse - they think you need to get out more because your life is so sad and empty!

jokes.text: No, it's just that he gave me three tickets and there are six of us.
jokes.setup: Previous sexual assumption
jokes.punchline: Reveals the actual mundane problem - not enough tickets
jokes.explanation: Good completion of the misdirection setup. Clean structure and good contrast.

dialogue.speaker: Character B
dialogue.line: Reveals the actual mundane problem - not enough tickets

jokes.text: And l think Ross is generous, too.
jokes.setup: Two people volunteer to give up tickets
jokes.punchline: Assumes Ross would also volunteer when he hasn't spoken
jokes.explanation: Mild character humor based on presuming Ross's generosity. Functional but not exceptional.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Okay, who wants to give up their tickets?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I will.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Me too.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Great! Ross, you're in!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Whoa, whoa, whoa. I didn't say anything!

jokes.text: lt's mostly just photographs of...lesbian love scenes interspersed with video games and free sandwiches.
jokes.setup: Describing what appears to be a legitimate art exhibit
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous combination of erotic art, gaming, and food
jokes.explanation: Absurd combination that's memorable and quotable. The escalation from art to complete randomness works well.

dialogue.speaker: Character B
dialogue.line: So I went to this art gallery opening last night. It was supposed to be this really sophisticated, high-brow exhibit. Very exclusive, very elite.

dialogue.speaker: Character B
dialogue.line: Turns out it was just a bunch of erotic paintings of people playing video games while eating sandwiches.

dialogue.speaker: Character B
dialogue.line: The artist called it 'Post-Modern Sustenance.' I called it a cry for help.

jokes.text: Oh, man!
jokes.setup: Description of lesbian photos, video games, and sandwiches
jokes.punchline: Disappointed reaction to missing out
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective reaction beat. Shows character priorities humorously.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Wait, wait, wait. You're telling me there were lesbian photos, video games, AND sandwiches, and nobody called me?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah, that's basically a Joey paradise.

jokes.text: Chandler got you out of going to the lesbian sandwich museum this weekend.
jokes.setup: Previous description of the exhibit
jokes.punchline: Reduces it to absurd shorthand that emphasizes the randomness
jokes.explanation: Good callback that distills the absurdity into a memorable phrase. Economy and wordplay work well.

jokes.text: Well, her father pays you for babysitting, right?
jokes.setup: Ross mentioning plans with Elizabeth and making money
jokes.punchline: Implies Ross is dating someone so young it's like babysitting
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific joke about Ross's age-inappropriate relationship. Well-constructed setup/punchline.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: So you're dating Elizabeth and making money? What's next, you're going to tell me you're babysitting her during the day?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: What? No, I'm not—

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Because that's what it sounds like, man. You're dating a girl so young you might as well be her nanny.

jokes.text: Looks like l'm not the only one interested in fossils.
jokes.setup: Learning Rachel is dating Elizabeth's older father
jokes.punchline: Uses his profession (paleontology) to make age joke
jokes.explanation: Excellent character integration using Ross's profession. Clever wordplay that only Ross could deliver.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: She's dating her father? That's insane! I mean, what is he, like, sixty?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Well, I'm a paleontologist. I date fossils.

jokes.text: Ross, all that does is remind us that you are interested in fossils.
jokes.setup: Ross's attempted clever comeback
jokes.punchline: Points out the joke doesn't work as intended
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Ross's attempted cleverness. Clean structure.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: So... the thing about that is... well, actually, it was a clever thing I said.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: No, it wasn't.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Yes, it was! You just didn't get it.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I got it. It just wasn't clever.

jokes.text: Rachel's gonna need to yell sweet nothings in his ear.
jokes.setup: Continuing to make jokes about the older man
jokes.punchline: Plays on 'whisper sweet nothings' but needs to yell due to hearing loss
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay on a common phrase. Structure works but not exceptional.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: He's so old, he doesn't whisper sweet nothings anymore. He has to yell sweet nothings because of his hearing loss!

jokes.text: 'Cause he's got like a hearing aid, you know? 'Cause he's all old.
jokes.setup: The yelling joke
jokes.punchline: Over-explains the already clear age joke
jokes.explanation: Weak over-explanation that kills the previous joke. Poor economy and structure.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: She's a high school student!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: So?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: So?! She's way too young for you! She's in high school, Chandler. High school! That means she goes to high school classes during the day, she has homework, she probably has a curfew—

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Okay, okay, I get it. She's young.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Do you? Because I don't think you understand how young she is. She wasn't even born when we started watching this show!

jokes.text: Yeah. But then l changed my mind. l'm funny like that.
jokes.setup: Paul saying he accepted Ross dating his daughter
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of being manipulative presented as quirky
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Paul's casual cruelty. Well-crafted understatement.

dialogue.speaker: Paul
dialogue.line: Look, I'm okay with you dating my daughter. I mean, I've been manipulating her entire life to get what I want, so at least now I know someone else is doing it too.

jokes.text: A man with a plan!
jokes.setup: Paul threatening to get Ross fired
jokes.punchline: Celebrates his threat with cheery enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Paul's satisfaction with his manipulation. Economical delivery.

dialogue.speaker: Paul
dialogue.line: I'm gonna get you fired, Ross!

jokes.text: Me and Jim Belushi would just be cracking up about something. Then l get fired off Days of Our Lives and he takes me down.
jokes.setup: Joey explaining his wall of fame history
jokes.punchline: Imagines intimate friendship with celebrity based on wall proximity
jokes.explanation: Great character comedy showing Joey's delusions. Good specificity with Jim Belushi.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I have pictures of all these celebrities on my wall, and the closer they are to each other, the closer friends I am with them.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Like, Brad Pitt and I? We're like *this* close. We practically live together.

jokes.text: Now he's just laughing at me. Look at him. That smug Belushi bastard!
jokes.setup: Joey's imagined friendship with Belushi
jokes.punchline: Turns photo into personal enemy with escalating anger
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of Joey's paranoia. 'Smug Belushi bastard' is memorably quotable.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Me and Belushi would've been best friends. We would've hung out, gone to parties together...

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Then he dies and I'm left without my best friend.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Why'd you have to leave me, Belushi? Why?!

jokes.text: Look at him just smiling at me.
jokes.setup: Joey considering Matt Lauer as photo neighbor
jokes.punchline: Anthropomorphizes another photo with sinister intent
jokes.explanation: Continues the photo paranoia theme but less impact than Belushi line.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Wait, Matt Lauer? He could be my photo neighbor!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: But then... who's gonna stop him from stealing my other photos while I sleep?

jokes.text: So, do you have any of Matt Lauer's clothes here? Maybe? Just ones that haven't been cleaned yet?
jokes.setup: Joey trying to get on the wall
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate request for unwashed celebrity clothes
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing Joey's inappropriate celebrity obsession. Very character-specific.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey, could you get me some of your unwashed clothes?

dialogue.speaker: Celebrity
dialogue.line: What? Why would I do that?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Well, I'm trying to get on the wall of fame, and I figured if I wore your dirty stuff, maybe some of your talent would rub off on me.

jokes.text: What do you think? Maybe he's gonna tell you he's gay?
jokes.setup: Rachel excited about Paul's big surprise
jokes.punchline: Completely random and devastating possibility
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection from romantic to devastating. Structure works well.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I can't wait to see what Paul's big surprise is!

jokes.text: No reason. That would just be a really big surprise, right?
jokes.setup: Rachel asking why Monica suggested he's gay
jokes.punchline: Maintains innocent tone while doubling down
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up maintaining the bit. Decent deadpan delivery.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: What? I didn't say he was gay. I just said he seemed... well-groomed.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Monica, you literally said—

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I said he had nice hair! Is it a crime to appreciate good hair maintenance?

jokes.text: There's a two-year wait. What if you get engaged in two years? Then you've gotta wait another two years? That's four years. Chandler's not gonna wait that long.
jokes.setup: Monica hesitant about wedding venue waiting list
jokes.punchline: Escalating panic about timeline logistics
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure showing Rachel's logic spiral. Character-appropriate anxiety.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: We need to get on the waiting list at that venue. It's the only place that feels right.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: How long is the waiting list?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Two years.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Two years? Monica, we're getting married in eight months.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I know!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: So... you're not getting married there.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: No, no, we're going on the list. People drop off the list all the time!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: People die, Mon.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Not just from death! Divorces, elopements, cold feet...

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: So you're banking on someone's relationship falling apart so you can have your wedding?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Yes! Is that wrong?

jokes.text: Don't feel too sorry for me. At least my boyfriend isn't gay.
jokes.setup: Phoebe admitting she has no one to marry
jokes.punchline: References Monica's earlier Paul comment
jokes.explanation: Good callback that escalates the gay boyfriend joke. Well-timed and character-appropriate.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I don't have anyone to marry. I have no one.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: What about Paul?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Paul?! He's not... I mean, we went out like twice!

jokes.text: Don't even get me started on yours.
jokes.setup: Monica trying to defend Paul
jokes.punchline: Implies Monica's boyfriend (Chandler) is even worse somehow
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with excellent economy. Typical Phoebe randomness.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Paul is not that bad!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Monica, he's a terrible person.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: No he's not! He's sweet and he's—

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Okay, fine. At least he's better than Chandler.

jokes.text: Dry cleaners?
jokes.setup: Russian dry cleaner saying Joey's show was offensive to 'my people'
jokes.punchline: Joey misunderstands what group he means
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection with perfect economy. Very Joey-like misunderstanding. Great setup/punchline structure.

dialogue.speaker: Russian Cleaner
dialogue.line: Your show was very offensive to my people.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh man, I'm sorry. I didn't mean to offend Russians.

dialogue.speaker: Russian Cleaner
dialogue.line: No, no. Actors.

jokes.text: l mean, not her actual eyeballs.
jokes.setup: Elizabeth saying she got her grandmother's eyes
jokes.punchline: Clarifies the obvious to avoid gruesome interpretation
jokes.explanation: Mild clarification humor. Standard structure but not exceptional.

dialogue.speaker: Elizabeth
dialogue.line: I got my grandmother's eyes.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: You mean you have the same color eyes as your grandmother?

dialogue.speaker: Elizabeth
dialogue.line: Yes.

jokes.text: Why? Are there like bears or something?
jokes.setup: Ross asking if she brought protection
jokes.punchline: Completely innocent interpretation of 'protection'
jokes.explanation: Good innocence comedy showing age gap. Clean misdirection structure.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Did you bring protection?

dialogue.speaker: Elizabeth
dialogue.line: Of course! I have an umbrella, my mace, and I parked under a light.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: That's not what I meant.

jokes.text: Do you want to ride around town on my little pink bicycle?
jokes.setup: Ross offering to go to the store instead
jokes.punchline: Reveals absurd transportation option emphasizing youth
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy emphasizing Elizabeth's youth. Character-specific and memorable.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: No, no, I'll go to the store.

dialogue.speaker: Elizabeth
dialogue.line: Are you sure?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Yeah, yeah, I'll take my skateboard.

jokes.text: A little bit.
jokes.setup: Elizabeth offering her pink bicycle
jokes.punchline: Ross honestly admits slight interest in riding it
jokes.explanation: Good honest reaction beat. Economy works well for the admission.

dialogue.speaker: Elizabeth
dialogue.line: You can ride my bike if you want.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Oh, um... well, maybe just a little bit. It does look pretty comfortable.

jokes.text: Hi, this is Hildy from the Morgan Chase Museum. I'm calling for Monica Geller. There's been a cancellation. If she's still interested in having the Bing-Geller wedding at our facility...
jokes.setup: Wedding venue calling with availability
jokes.punchline: Chandler hearing about wedding plans he doesn't know about
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic irony setup. Standard structure for sitcom misunderstanding.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Hello?

dialogue.speaker: Hildy
dialogue.line: Hi, is this Chandler Bing? This is Hildy from the Pierre Hotel. I'm calling about your wedding reception.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: My wedding reception? I don't have a wedding reception.

dialogue.speaker: Hildy
dialogue.line: Well, according to our records, you have a reservation for 200 people.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: 200 people? I can't even get 200 people to like me!

jokes.text: This is Chandler Bing! Yes, the groom! No, not the groom!
jokes.setup: Chandler answering call about his wedding
jokes.punchline: Rapid emotional journey from acceptance to panic
jokes.explanation: Good panic comedy showing Chandler's commitment phobia. Nice escalation structure.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Hello? Yes, this is he. Oh, great! So the flowers are definitely going to be there? Wonderful. And the caterer confirmed? Perfect. You know what? Everything is coming together beautifully. I am so excited about this wedding. I cannot wait to— wait, what? The what is broken? How is that possible? When?! But the wedding is in three days! No, no, no, this is a disaster. This is an absolute disaster!

jokes.text: Yeah. l recognized the ankles.
jokes.setup: Ross asking what Rachel is doing at Paul's house
jokes.punchline: Identifies her by ankles visible under hot tub
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor with excellent economy. Specific and memorable visual.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: What are you doing at Paul's house?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I'm in the hot tub.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I can see that... I'd recognize those ankles anywhere.

jokes.text: Well, she obviously saw the tire tracks that were leading up to the closed garage.
jokes.setup: Elizabeth asking how they knew she was there
jokes.punchline: Overly detailed explanation for obvious observation
jokes.explanation: Weak over-explanation humor. Poor economy and not particularly clever.

dialogue.speaker: Elizabeth
dialogue.line: How did you guys know I was here?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Well, we heard the sound of a car door closing outside, and then we heard footsteps on the stairs, and then we heard you unlock the door with your key, and then we heard you come inside and say 'Hi, I'm here!'

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Or we could've just seen you through the window.

jokes.text: That's your dad's bedroom!
jokes.setup: Elizabeth mentioning not doing homework in dad's bedroom
jokes.punchline: Ross realizes he was just in there with Rachel
jokes.explanation: Standard realization comedy. Functional but not exceptional.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Wait, you weren't doing homework in your dad's bedroom?

dialogue.speaker: Elizabeth
dialogue.line: No, I was in there with Rachel.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Oh my God.

jokes.text: That Diet Coke, l think, went straight to my head!
jokes.setup: Elizabeth asking why he's yelling
jokes.punchline: Blames caffeine-free drink for bizarre behavior
jokes.explanation: Decent panic comedy with absurd excuse. Standard sitcom panic behavior.

dialogue.speaker: Elizabeth
dialogue.line: Why are you yelling?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I'm not yelling! I had a caffeine-free drink and now I'm all jittery and weird!

jokes.text: You are the man! You are the man!
jokes.setup: Paul giving himself pep talk in mirror
jokes.punchline: Self-affirmation ritual before romantic encounter
jokes.explanation: Standard cringe comedy. Character-appropriate but not innovative.

dialogue.speaker: Paul
dialogue.line: You can do this. You're a good-looking guy. You're smart, you're funny, you're... you're gonna kill it out there.

jokes.text: I'm just a love machine
jokes.setup: Paul continuing self-affirmation
jokes.punchline: Singing to boost confidence
jokes.explanation: Weak cringe comedy relying on song reference. Limited craft.

dialogue.speaker: Paul
dialogue.line: I'm a worthy person. I deserve good things. I'm going to get back out there and find love.

dialogue.speaker: Paul
dialogue.line: ♪ I'm great, I'm great, I'm really really great ♪

jokes.text: Well, with a regular guy it's bad. With Chandler, oh, dear God!
jokes.setup: Phoebe asking how bad the wedding mix-up is
jokes.punchline: Implies Chandler's commitment issues make it catastrophic
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific escalation. Shows understanding of Chandler's psychology.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: How bad is it?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Well, Chandler's commitment issues just ruined the entire wedding.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh my God, that's terrible!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Yeah, it's like the universe knew exactly how to hurt us.

jokes.text: Well, Rachel's not here!
jokes.setup: Phoebe saying Rachel suggested putting name down
jokes.punchline: Blames absent person in panic
jokes.explanation: Standard panic deflection. Functional but not exceptional.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Rachel said I should put my name down.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: For what?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I don't know, she didn't say. But now I'm freaking out because I don't know what I signed up for!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Well, just ask Rachel.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I can't! She's not here! This is all her fault!

jokes.text: Joey, why did you sign it, 'son of a bitch'?
jokes.setup: Looking at Joey's signature
jokes.punchline: Someone vandalized his autograph with insult
jokes.explanation: Decent visual gag setup for Joey's problems with the dry cleaner.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh my God, someone wrote 'washed up' right over your signature!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: What? No! That was me!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: You signed your autographs 'washed up'?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I was being ironic!

jokes.text: l did not go out with your wife! Okay? l went out with her.
jokes.setup: Dry cleaner accusing Joey of going out with his wife
jokes.punchline: Joey denies then immediately confirms the same thing
jokes.explanation: Excellent Joey logic confusion. Perfect character integration showing his thought process.

dialogue.speaker: Dry Cleaner
dialogue.line: You go out with my wife!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: No, no, I don't even know your wife.

dialogue.speaker: Dry Cleaner
dialogue.line: That's my wife!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh! OH! Yeah, yeah, I went out with her. She's a very nice woman.

jokes.text: What you heard was: 'Take my daughter...come up to my country house and ruin my weekend with Rachel.'
jokes.setup: Paul recapping his threat to Ross
jokes.punchline: Sarcastically interprets Ross's actions as malicious plan
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic escalation. Shows Paul's anger building effectively.

dialogue.speaker: Paul
dialogue.line: So let me get this straight. You're saying that Ross deliberately tried to ruin my life by... what, exactly? Having a relationship with Rachel?

dialogue.speaker: Paul
dialogue.line: Oh yes, that's it. That was his master plan all along. He thought, 'You know what Paul needs? Some emotional turmoil. Let me date his ex-girlfriend to really stick it to him.'

jokes.text: ...l, too, am a 'neat guy.'
jokes.setup: Ross trying to relate to Paul
jokes.punchline: References Paul's private self-affirmation
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Paul's embarrassing moment. Creates awkward connection.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: So Paul, I totally get it. I have a private self-affirmation too.

dialogue.speaker: Paul
dialogue.line: You do?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Yeah, every morning I look in the mirror and I say, 'You're a paleontologist, and that's cool.'

jokes.text: And l, too...am 'just a love machine.'
jokes.setup: Ross continuing to reference overheard private moment
jokes.punchline: Quotes Paul's embarrassing song
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of the callback. Creates maximum embarrassment for Paul and shows Ross's desperation.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Oh, you mean like when Paul was singing in the shower? 'I'm a little teapot, short and stout, here is my handle, here is my spout'?

jokes.text: So the people that knew about our wedding before me were you...Phoebe, Rachel, Hildy, and some band called, Star Light Magic Seven...who are available, by the way.
jokes.setup: Chandler discovering Monica planned wedding without him
jokes.punchline: Lists increasingly absurd people who knew first, including available band
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation structure with great specificity. The band detail is perfect Chandler sarcasm.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: You planned our wedding without me? Who else knows?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Well, my mother, your mother, Rachel, Phoebe, Joey...

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: The guys at the coffee shop? The woman who cuts my hair? That guy who sells hot dogs on the corner?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: No, Chandler—

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Because I'm pretty sure I saw a flyer at the post office. And I think the available band knows.

jokes.text: Do you still work here? No. l quit a long time ago. Did l forget to tell you that? l'm sorry.
jokes.setup: Gunther giving Ross work orders
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation that he quit long ago
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery with typical Ross social awkwardness. Solid structure.

dialogue.speaker: Gunther
dialogue.line: Here are your tables for tonight, Ross.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Wait, Gunther, I quit three months ago.

dialogue.speaker: Gunther
dialogue.line: I know.

jokes.text: That's cool. l was gonna fire you anyway.
jokes.setup: Ross revealing he quit
jokes.punchline: Gunther maintains power with casual dismissal
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Gunther. Excellent economy and typical deadpan style.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I quit.

dialogue.speaker: Gunther
dialogue.line: Yeah, that's fine.

tmdb_overview: Chandler and Phoebe shop for a ring for Monica. Chandler finds the perfect one, but Phoebe accidentally lets it get away. While Chandler is busy with getting the ring back, Joey and Ross are lead to believe that he's mad at them, so they decide to team up and give him a cold shoulder treatment. Rachel continues to date Paul and wants him to open up to her, however, he opens up a bit too much.
insight: Season finale falls flat at 64—character comedy can't overcome weak misdirection payoffs.
